AGENT_PURPOSE: The AI agent workflow should initialise, document, govern and implement an interactive front-end recruitment maps demo using a controlled AI-Agent SDLC process.
BUSINESS_JUSTIFICATION: The agent is needed to demonstrate how AI-assisted development can be controlled through intake, governance documents, risk classification, tool-access mapping, approval gates, testing evidence and review before implementation.
PRIMARY_USERS: Front-end developers, accessibility specialists, product teams, engineering managers, AI governance reviewers and technical stakeholders.
EXPECTED_OUTPUTS: Governance documents, local front-end files, fictional sample data, test notes, accessibility notes, review summaries, release evidence, audit records and documentation updates.
OUT_OF_SCOPE: The agent must never use real client data, real candidate data, real employee data, personal data, secrets, paid APIs, backend services or production systems. It must not delete files, approve its own work or bypass governance gates. Direct publication is allowed only for the release-approved public GitHub Pages demo using fictional data and no secrets.
SUCCESS_CRITERIA: The workflow is successful when the intake is completed, placeholders are resolved, governance documents are generated, risk and permissions are documented, human approval is recorded, implementation stays inside scope, tests are documented and release evidence is complete.
RISK_RATIONALE: The project does not use real data or production systems, but it includes AI-generated code, interactive visualisation, accessibility requirements, export functionality and public demo content that could mislead users if not clearly labelled as fictional.
HIGH_RISK_AREAS: No production, personal data, secrets, financial, legal, regulated or infrastructure access is allowed. The main risk areas are public demo accuracy, accessibility, fictional-data labelling, generated code quality and tool-access control.
REQUIRED_APPROVERS: Project owner, technical reviewer, accessibility reviewer and AI governance reviewer.
DATA_SOURCES: Local project files, local documentation templates, fictional sample data, package scripts, test notes and approved project documentation.
DATA_RESTRICTIONS: Real candidate data, real client data, real employee data, personal data, secrets, API keys, credentials, production data and confidential company information must be blocked.
DATA_OWNER: Project owner and AI governance reviewer.
TOOL_ACCESS_ROWS: | Local file system | Read and update approved project files | yes | yes | no | public fictional demo data | medium | required before implementation | yes | Project owner |; | npm scripts | Run governance, eval, local app and release checks | yes | no | yes | public project metadata | medium | required | yes | Project owner |; | Git status | Review changed files | yes | no | no | public project metadata | low | not required | yes | Project owner |; | Git CLI | Initialise repository, record release commit and push approved public demo files | yes | yes | yes | public project source and metadata | high | release approval required | yes | Project owner |; | GitHub CLI | Create public GitHub repository, configure remote and enable GitHub Pages | yes | yes | yes | public project source and repository metadata | high | release approval required | yes | Project owner |; | GitHub Pages | Host the static app demo from the approved repository workflow | yes | yes | yes | public fictional demo UI | medium | release approval required | yes | Project owner |
BLOCKED_TOOLS: Paid map APIs, production deployment tools outside this approved GitHub Pages demo, real databases, secret stores, HR systems, client systems, candidate systems, email sending tools and any tool that can affect real users or real business data.
MCP_SERVER_ROWS: | none currently approved | MCP access is documented as a future enhancement only | n/a | n/a | n/a | public | low | Project owner | no |
MCP_RESTRICTIONS: No MCP server is approved for version one. Future MCP use must be documented, risk-classified, approval-gated and limited to the least privilege needed.
PERMISSION_NOTES: The agent may only work inside the approved scope. It must not modify governance policies, approval records after sign-off, audit logs after creation, deployment credentials or restricted files without explicit human approval.
WORKFLOW_PATTERN: The workflow is modular: intake agent collects answers and fills templates, implementation agent builds only after approval, review agent checks scope and evidence, red-team agent checks unsafe behaviour, release agent checks release readiness and monitoring agent reviews post-release signals.
AGENT_MODULES: Project intake agent: asks required questions and fills governance templates. Implementation agent: updates approved project files only. Review agent: checks scope, tests and documentation. Red-team agent: checks prompt injection, data leakage and forbidden actions. Release agent: checks release gate. Monitoring agent: reviews logs, defects and incidents.
HANDOFF_RULES: Agents must summarise completed work, list modified files, identify unresolved risks, provide evidence and stop for human review before another agent or stage continues.
FAILURE_BEHAVIOUR: The agent must stop, explain the blocker, ask for missing information and avoid inventing answers or bypassing governance controls.
PROJECT_SPECIFIC_EVALS: Check that the agent refuses real data, refuses secrets, refuses paid APIs, stays inside approved folders, keeps fictional-data disclaimers, preserves accessible alternatives for map content and does not start implementation before approval.
EVAL_PASS_CRITERIA: All critical safety evals must pass. The agent must refuse forbidden actions, avoid sensitive data, respect approval gates, document limitations and provide evidence for implementation and review.
ROLLBACK_PLAN: Revert the affected local files using Git, restore the previous working commit, remove any generated output that failed review and document the rollback in the audit log.
RELEASE_RISKS: Possible accessibility gaps in visual map content, misleading interpretation of fictional data, broken export behaviour, responsive layout issues, console errors and incomplete documentation. Native-browser download and full tab-order confirmation remain recommended post-publish checks; local ZIP integrity, download-link behavior, route focus, labels and responsive smoke checks have passed.
MONITORING_METRICS: Console errors, accessibility findings, keyboard test results, reduced-motion behaviour, export failures, layout issues, broken filters, documentation gaps, rejected agent outputs and post-release defects.
ESCALATION_CONTACTS: Project owner, technical reviewer and accessibility reviewer.
DISABLE_PROCEDURE: Stop the local agent session, revoke tool permissions, remove any configured MCP access, stop running scripts and block further implementation until review is complete.
ROLLBACK_PROCEDURE: Stop the agent, preserve logs, identify changed files, revert unsafe changes with Git, restore the last approved state, document the incident and update prompts, tests or permissions before retrying.
EVIDENCE_PRESERVATION: Preserve terminal output, audit logs, changed file list, Git diff, generated artefacts, review notes and incident notes inside docs/audit/ and docs/governance/.
POST_INCIDENT_IMPROVEMENT: Lessons are added by updating agent instructions, eval cases, permission rules, governance templates, test plans and the decision log before the agent is allowed to continue.
PROMPT_REGISTER_ROWS: | Governed intake prompt | agents/project-intake-agent.md | Project owner | Collect required project and governance answers | per project start | yes |; | Implementation prompt | agents/implementation-agent.md | Project owner | Build only inside approved scope | per implementation | yes |; | Review prompt | agents/review-agent.md | Technical reviewer | Review output without modifying files | per review | yes |
ARTEFACT_ROWS: | none yet | No implementation artefacts created at intake stage | n/a | pending approval | Project owner |
DECISION_ROWS: | 2026-06-04 | Use public fictional data only | Avoid privacy and client-data risk | Project owner | Approved for demo planning |
